name: Pedraza and by the Río Cega — circular by Cantalejo
name_original: Pedraza y por el Río Cega — circular por Cantalejo
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pedraza offer diverse terrain in Spain's Castile and León region. The area features a mix of landscapes, from the hilltop village providing panoramic views to the varied terrain of the Sierra de Guadarrama Park. Cyclists can expect a combination of flat sections, valleys, and challenging climbs through mountain passes. The region's geology, characterized by sandstones and limestone, contributes to its distinct appearance. Best road cycling routes around Pedraza The most popular road cycling route is Pedraza and by the Río Cega — circular by Cantalejo, a 47.6 miles (76.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 20 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers scenic views along the Río Cega. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Navafría Pass – Puerto de Navafría loop from Gallegos, a difficult 30.6 miles (49.2 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain through mountain passes. Local road cyclists also love the Canencia Pass – Navafría Pass loop from Pedraza, a 74.4 miles (119.7 km) trail leading through multiple mountain passes, often completed in about 6 hours 17 minutes. Road cycling around Pedraza is defined by varied terrain including valleys, mountain passes, and open countryside. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to challenging climbs. The routes in Pedraza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 300 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pedraza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pedraza?
A: There are over 25 road cycling routes around Pedraza, offering a variety of experiences. These include 15 moderate routes and 11 difficult routes, catering to different skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Pedraza?
A: The region around Pedraza offers diverse terrain. You can expect a mix of well-paved surfaces, challenging climbs through mountain passes, and flat sections winding through valleys and open countryside. The area's geology, with its sandstones and limestone, contributes to a distinct and varied landscape.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in the Pedraza area?
A: While Pedraza's cobbled streets are not ideal for road cycling, the surrounding area does offer routes suitable for various abilities. For a less strenuous option, consider exploring some of the moderate routes, though specific 'family-friendly' designations for road cycling are less common than for leisure cycling. Always check the route's difficulty and elevation profile before setting out with family.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Pedraza?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Pedraza are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064521423/pedraza-and-by-the-rio-cega-circular-by-cantalejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedraza and by the Río Cega — circular by Cantalejo&lt;/a&gt; route, which offers scenic views along the Río Cega.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I can see while cycling near Pedraza?
A: While cycling the quiet secondary roads around Pedraza, you can enjoy views of the medieval village itself, perched on a hilltop. Other attractions in the broader area include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2347605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de Linera&lt;/a&gt; mountain pass, and various viewpoints like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5376252" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador de los Castillejos&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6741653" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillejo Bajero Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, offering panoramic vistas of the countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in the Pedraza region?
A: The region of Castile and León, where Pedraza is located, generally offers pleasant cycling conditions during spring and autumn. These seasons provide milder temperatures, making longer rides more comfortable. Summer can be quite warm, especially in the midday, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially less predictable weather.

Q: Are there options for advanced road cyclists seeking a challenge?
A: Absolutely. The Pedraza area is known for its challenging climbs and mountain passes. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38216699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Navafría Pass – Puerto de Navafría loop from Gallegos&lt;/a&gt; and the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33728274" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass – Navafría Pass loop from Pedraza&lt;/a&gt; feature significant elevation gains and are popular among experienced cyclists.

Q: Where can I find parking for my car when starting a road cycling route in Pedraza?
A: While Pedraza's historic center has limited access, parking is typically available just outside the walled village. Look for designated parking areas near the main entrance, the Puerta de la Villa, which serve as convenient starting points for exploring the surrounding road cycling routes.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or natural features to discover on the routes?
A: Yes, the natural landscape around Pedraza includes some beautiful features. You might encounter natural highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3348944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mill and Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4138646" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cancho Litero Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, offering picturesque spots to pause during your ride.

Q: What do other cyclists say about road cycling in Pedraza?
A: The road cycling routes around Pedraza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the quiet secondary roads, and the stunning views of the Mediterranean landscape and picturesque villages.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for cycling in natural parks like Duratón Canyon?
A: For cycling within specific protected areas, such as the Duratón Canyon Natural Park, it is advisable to check local regulations regarding permits or restricted access. While general road cycling on public roads usually doesn't require permits, natural parks may have specific rules to protect wildlife and natural habitats.
tips: San Pedro de Gaíllos is a town located at the entrance to the great plains in the north of the province of Segovia. The town, with its church as its flag, governs the core.

It is located at the foot of the road that connects La Velilla and Val de San Pedro, close to the Requijada neighborhood, which together with other towns that no longer exist today, made up what was known as the Las Vegas council.
One of the most impressive towns in the south of the Sierra de Guadarrama and which consists of a castle that rises above a hilly area. You can already see the silhouette of the town from afar!
